name: Surullitos (Cheese Corn Sticks)
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Freshly grated, good quality cheese (Edam, Gouda, or Cheddar)
1/4 teaspoon Cayenne pepper, or to taste (optional)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the water and salt in a heavy saucepan and bring to a boil over high heat.
2. Add the cornmeal in a slow, constant stream, stirring constantly, until the mixture is smooth and thick. Continue cooking over medium heat for 2 to 3 minutes, stirring constantly, until the mixture is smooth and thick.
3. Remove from the heat and stir in the grated cheese and optional cayenne pepper. Cool to room temperature.
4. Roll about 30 ml (2 tablespoons) of the mixture into cylinders about 7.5 cm long and 2.5 cm thick. Moistening your hands with water will help prevent the dough from sticking to your fingers.
5. Refrigerate the surullitos, covered with plastic wrap or wax paper, for up to a day or two.
6. Heat oil in a heavy skillet over high heat until it shimmers (about 350°F/180°C).
7. Fry the surullitos 4-5 at a time for about 5 minutes, turning occasionally, until they are crisp and golden brown.
8. Drain on paper towels. Keep warm by placing on paper towels in an oven set to its lowest setting if needed. Serve hot or at room temperature.
